_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-national autopilot)
_meta.note: Federal House batch: FL-03 Cammack / FL-04 Bean / FL-05 Rutherford / FL-06 Fine / FL-08 Haridopolos. All five web-verified as sitting members running for re-election 2026 (Ballotpedia, Aug 18 2026 primaries). Key deviation catches: Cammack voted YES on the Respect for Marriage Act twice (one of 39 GOP final-passage yeas) and took AIPAC PAC money; Bean and Fine both voted FOR Florida's 2018 SB 7026 red-flag law; Bean voted NAY on the Iran war-powers resolution; Haridopolos presided over the death of mandatory E-Verify as FL Senate President (2011). Scored from House Clerk roll calls, GovTrack cosponsor lists, Heritage Action, SBA Pro-Life, NRA-PVF, OpenSecrets/FEC, FL Senate vote PDFs.

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 from Clerk roll calls (117th-119th), Heritage Action (94% lifetime), SBA Pro-Life (A+), OpenSecrets. Re-election bid 2026 web-verified (Ballotpedia, GOP primary 8/18/2026). Religion not reliably documented — left blank.

kat-cammack.notes_set: FL-03 (Gainesville/Ocala) since 2021. Heritage Action 94% lifetime / 91% (118th); SBA Pro-Life A+. DEVIATIONS: voted YES on the Respect for Marriage Act in both July 2022 (Roll 373) and Dec 2022 (Roll 513) — one of 39 GOP final-passage yeas, calling it 'the constitutional vote'; accepted $25,360 in AIPAC-affiliated pro-Israel PAC money (OpenSecrets/FEC). Pro-life record is restriction-tier (15-week ban cosponsor, Born-Alive yea) — no documented abolition advocacy; in 2024 she publicly recounted delayed ectopic-pregnancy treatment under FL's abortion law, blaming 'fearmongering' rather than the law. One of 49 GOP yeas to repeal the 2002 Iraq AUMF (Roll 172, 2021); voted against later Ukraine military aid but for Lend-Lease — mixed restraint signal. Cosponsored H.R.38 concealed-carry reciprocity (119th) — reciprocity, not permitless carry, so constitutional-carry cell left null.

aaron-bean.notes_set: FL-04 (Jacksonville) since 2023; FL Senate 2012-2022, FL House 2000-2008; chairs Early Childhood/K-12 subcommittee. Heritage Action 92% (119th). DEVIATIONS: voted YEA on FL SB 7026 (Marjory Stoneman Douglas Act, 3/5/2018) — the red-flag/risk-protection-order law with 21-year purchase age (NRA downgraded him to 'B,' no endorsement, 2022); voted NAY on the Iran war-powers resolution (H.Con.Res.38, 2026); $72,587 career AIPAC/pro-Israel-lobby PAC money (FEC). Strong on life (Life at Conception Act cosponsor) and school choice (Educational Choice for Children Act champion). Sponsored FL SB 8B (2021) stripping the surgeon general's forced-vaccination power. FL E-Verify yea (SB 664, 2020) was the watered version — public employers only.

john-rutherford.notes_set: FL-05 (Jacksonville) since 2017; former Duval County Sheriff. Heritage Action lifetime 68% (118th session 55%, 119th 72%) — notably low for a Republican, driven by votes for bipartisan spending packages. SOFT DEVIATION on guns: said 'I support these red flag laws' (WJCT, 8/21/2019) — but his roll calls cut the other way (NAY on H.R.2377 federal ERPO, NAY on the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act, NAY on H.R.8 background checks), so the votes carry the cell. Voted NAY on RFMA twice and NAY on the Equality Act. Yea on the $61B Ukraine supplemental (Roll 151, 4/2024). Opposes capital punishment citing his Catholic faith. Cosponsored national concealed-carry reciprocity — reciprocity, not constitutional carry.

randy-fine.notes_set: FL-06 since the April 2025 special election (replacing Mike Waltz); FL House 2016-2023, FL Senate 2024-2025; House Foreign Affairs. Self-styled 'Hebrew Hammer' — authored FL's IHRA antisemitism-definition law (HB 741, 2019, signed in Jerusalem). DEVIATIONS: supported FL SB 7026 (2018 red-flag + age-21 law), telling NPR he'd 'absolutely' accept a lower NRA grade — later filed a bill repealing the under-21 purchase ban; ~$458K in AIPAC-network money flooded his 2025 special (Evanston Now/Sludge). Strong FL record on minors' gender-care ban (sponsor), anti-drag law, HB 1 universal vouchers (co-introducer), HB 543 permitless carry (co-introducer), school moment-of-silence law (sponsor). Abortion record is restriction-tier (sponsored the 15-week ban, yea on the 6-week ban) — no documented abolition stance. Hawkish-interventionist posture (Greenland Annexation and Statehood Act, Jan 2026) noted; opposed one Ukraine aid bill on the floor. Heritage Action: no session score yet at research time.

mike-haridopolos.notes_set: FL-08 (Space Coast) since 2025 (replacing Bill Posey); FL Senate President 2010-2012. Thin federal record — scored from 119th-Congress marquee roll calls (verified in Clerk XML) and cosponsor lists; many cells honestly null. DEVIATION: mandatory-E-Verify immigration reform died under his FL Senate presidency in 2011 (the Senate stripped private-employer E-Verify; PolitiFact). NOT a Life at Conception Act (H.R.722) cosponsor — absence confirmed on GovTrack, but SBA Pro-Life Candidate Fund endorsed him in 2024. Yea on OBBBA both passes (deficit-increasing reconciliation) — mixed fiscal signal; the 'fiscal conservatism book' claim is false (his book was a $152K taxpayer-funded Brevard CC contract that became an ethics controversy). Cosponsored the Birthright Citizenship Act (H.R.569) and Anti-CBDC Act (H.R.1919). NRA-PVF 'A' + endorsement 2024. Cosponsored H.R.38 reciprocity — not constitutional carry. As Senate President, credited by Florida Family Action for the 2011 ultrasound law's passage.
